mission.title: Install and Configure Grafana Operator
mission.description: The Grafana Operator simplifies the management of Grafana instances, dashboards, and data sources in Kubernetes. It is ideal for users who prefer infrastructure as code or GitOps workflows. Key trade-offs include the need for familiarity with Kubernetes custom resources and the potential complexity of managing multiple Grafana instances.

steps.title: Install the Grafana Operator
steps.description:
This command installs the Grafana Operator in your Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm upgrade -i grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

```

> Installs the Grafana Operator with the specified version.

steps.title: Create a Grafana instance
steps.description:
This step creates a Grafana instance using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
EOF

```

> Creates a Grafana instance with basic configuration.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: Grafana
metadata:
  name: grafana
  labels:
    dashboards: "grafana"
spec:
  config:
    log:
      mode: "console"
    security:
      admin_user: root
      admin_password: secret
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Grafana instance
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the Grafana instance to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Displays the details of the Grafana instance.

steps.title: Deploy a sample dashboard
steps.description:
This step deploys a sample Grafana dashboard using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: GrafanaDashboard
metadata:
  name: sample-dashboard
spec:
  resyncPeriod: 30s
  instanceSelector:
    matchLabels:
      dashboards: "grafana"
  json: >
    {
      "title": "Simple Dashboard",
      "timezone": "browser",
      "refresh": "5s",
      "panels": [],
      "time": {
        "from": "now-6h",
        "to": "now"
      }
    }
EOF

```

> Creates a sample dashboard in the Grafana instance.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1
kind: GrafanaDashboard
metadata:
  name: sample-dashboard
spec:

  instanceSelector:

      dashboards: "grafana"
  json: >
    {
      "title": "Simple Dashboard",
      "timezone": "browser",
      "refresh": "5s",
      "panels": [],
      "time": {
        "from": "now-6h",
        "to": "now"
      }
    }
EOF

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Grafana Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Grafana Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss if Grafana instances are not backed up.

```bash

helm uninstall grafana-operator

kubectl delete grafana grafana

```

> Uninstalls the Grafana Operator from the cluster.
> Deletes the Grafana instance.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Grafana Operator
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up your Grafana instances. This step will upgrade the Grafana Operator to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

helm upgrade grafana-operator oci://ghcr.io/grafana/helm-charts/grafana-operator --version 5.22.2

kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml

```

> Backs up the current Grafana instance configuration.
> Upgrades the Grafana Operator to the specified version.
> Verifies that the Grafana instance is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get grafana grafana -o yaml > grafana-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Grafana" in version "grafana.integreatly.org/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Grafana CRD is not installed or not recognized.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the Grafana Operator is installed and running properly.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: failed to create Grafana: the server could not find the requested resource'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Grafana instance is not defined correctly or the operator is not managing it.

**Fix:**
Check the Grafana instance definition and ensure the operator is running.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: unable to connect to the Grafana instance'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the Grafana service is not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Verify the service configuration and ensure it is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Error: Grafana instance not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Grafana instance may not be fully initialized or there may be issues with the configuration.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Grafana Operator and the Grafana instance for errors.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new Grafana features and improved CRD handling.
versionNotes.deprecations: No specific deprecations noted in this version.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Grafana instance running with the status 'Running' and the sample dashboard available in the Grafana UI.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with the necessary resources.
